sc: Mrs. Levy, 79, daughter: 'keeps forgetting, got lost driving.' Patient: 'I\'m fine.' Perform cognitive assessment.
Mr. Cohen, 83, 3rd fall, wrist fracture. Meds: amlodipine, furosemide, temazepam, metoprolol.
Mrs. Azoulay, 88, advanced dementia CDR 3, recurrent aspiration PNA, 3rd admission. Family: 'do everything.'
Mr. Haim, 81, 12 meds incl oxybutynin, zolpidem, glimepiride. A1c 6.1%, BP 110/60, eGFR 35.
Mr. Baruch, 84, post-op day 2 hip fracture repair. Nurses report he's agitated, pulling at lines, hallucinating. Meds: tramadol, oxycodone, diphenhydramine PRN, amlodipine. Cr 1.8.
ck: Take structured history from nursing staff
Check for acute precipitants (infection, retention, constipation, pain)
Order workup (CBC, BMP, UA, CXR, glucose)
Medication management if needed (low-dose haloperidol with caveats)

sc: Mrs. Naomi, 77, presents with 6 months worsening incontinence. Takes furosemide 40mg, donepezil 10mg. Lives alone, restricting fluids, avoiding outings. PHQ-2 positive.
ck: Impact on QoL and social isolation
Rule out UTI, retention, fecal impaction

sc: Mr. Eli, 82, unintentional 8kg weight loss over 4 months. BMI 18.5. Lives with wife who has dementia. Takes 14 medications. Albumin 2.8, recent hospitalization for PNA.
ck: Quantify weight loss timeline and severity
Dietary history (24hr recall, who prepares meals)
Assess social situation (caregiver burden — wife has dementia)
Assess for sarcopenia (grip strength, gait speed)
Lab workup (albumin, prealbumin, B12, folate, TSH)

sc: Mrs. Dvora, 86, severe Alzheimer's (MMSE 8). Nursing home reports increased agitation, hitting staff, refusing care. No verbal complaints of pain. PMH: OA bilateral knees, compression fractures.
ck: Recognize behavior as possible pain expression
Use validated tool (PAINAD or Abbey scale)
Assess during movement and care activities
Trial of analgesia as diagnostic tool
Distinguish pain from other causes of agitation
Avoid chemical restraints as first line

sc: Mrs. Leah, 80, admitted 10 days ago with CHF exacerbation + fall. Stabilized on new meds. Lives alone, 2nd floor walk-up. Daughter works full-time. Was independent pre-admission, now needs walker.
ck: Assess current functional status vs baseline

sc: Mr. Yosef's family meeting. 91yo with advanced dementia, recurrent aspiration PNA, now with new lung mass on CXR. Daughter is sole decision-maker, previously requested 'do everything.' Son lives abroad, joining by phone.
ck: Set up environment properly (private, seated, phone connected)
Ask what family understands about current situation
Use empathic statements (NURSE: Name, Understand, Respect, Support, Explore)
Explore what 'do everything' means to them
